 {"id":"gt-fqcz","title":"Add gate timeout tracking and notification","description":"Implement timeout and notification logic for gates.

## Timeout Behavior
1. Gate created with timeout (e.g., 30m)
2. Deacon tracks elapsed time during patrol
3. If timeout reached:
   - Notify all waiters: 'Gate timed out'
   - Close gate with timeout reason
   - Waiter can retry, escalate, or fail gracefully

## Notification
- Use gt mail send to notify waiters
- Include gate ID, await type, and reason in message
- Support multiple waiters notification

## Moved from beads
Originally bd-ykqu. Gate notifications are Deacon's job in gastown.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:18.268978-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:18.268978-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-fqcz","depends_on_id":"gt-dh65","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:32.873641-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"gt-frs","title":"Polecat name pooling: Bounded reusable names","description":"Polecats reuse names from a bounded pool (50) with overflow to sequence numbers.

## Naming Scheme
- Pool: polecat-01 through polecat-50 (prefer low numbers)
- Overflow: <rigname>-<sequenceNumber> (e.g., beads-51, gastown-52)

## Design
- Witness tracks which pool names are in use
- On spawn: pick first available from pool
- If pool exhausted: use rigname-N format
- On completion: pool name returns, sequence numbers don't

## Why?
- User experience: tmux sessions survive polecat restarts
- Users stay attached, see new polecat start (like mayor respawn loop)
- Bounded resource usage for common case
- Scales beyond 50 when needed

## Implementation
- Witness maintains name allocation in beads or local state
- Tmux session runs respawn loop (like mayor)
- Name released on graceful exit or when witness detects dead session","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-18T18:32:28.43866-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:57.110202-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:57.110202-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"feature"}

 {"id":"gt-nq6j","title":"Rename .beads-ephemeral to .beads-wisps in docs","description":"The wisp storage directory should be named `.beads-wisps/` not `.beads-ephemeral/`.

Also fix the architecture: Witness and Refinery share mayor/rig's beads and wisps.
They don't have separate ephemeral stores.

## Files to update
- docs/wisp-architecture.md
- docs/architecture.md  
- ~/gt/docs/patrol-system-design.md
- ~/gt/CLAUDE.md

## Changes
1. Rename `.beads-ephemeral/` → `.beads-wisps/` everywhere
2. Remove incorrect references to witness/.beads-ephemeral/ and refinery/rig/.beads-ephemeral/
3. Clarify that all rig-level patrols use mayor/rig/.beads-wisps/","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:38:04.110859-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.358466-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:53.358466-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
 {"id":"gt-nqrh","title":"Auto-restart patrol agents after N loops","description":"All patrol roles should auto-restart to keep context usage low. Role-specific heuristics:

**Deacon**: 
- 20 patrol loops without major incident
- Immediate restart after ANY extraordinary action (lifecycle request, remediation, escalation)
- Rationale: Keep context short so there's headroom if something big comes up

**Witness**:
- Based on polecats processed (not loop count)
- Restart after processing N polecats (spawns, nudges, decommissions)
- Suggested N: 10-15 polecats

**Refinery**:
- Based on MRs processed (not loop count)  
- Restart after processing N merge requests
- Suggested N: 5-10 MRs (merges are context-heavy)

Benefits:
- Keeps context fresh (better focus/performance)
- Reduces cost (quadratic increase vs conversation length)
- Preserves headroom for handling surprises
- Simple and predictable (no context estimation needed)

Each role tracks their metric in state file and hands off when threshold reached.","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T14:00:00.093148-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.990581-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-27T21:29:52.990581-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task"}
